Fern Porrrrcal PnacrrcEs CouvrrssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 e Fax (916) 322-0886
January 20,2004
Vigo G. Nielsen, Jr.
Nielsen, Merksamer, Parrinello,
Mueller & Naylor, LLP
591 Redwood Highway, #4000
Mill Valley, CA 94941-3039
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-03-255
Dear Mr. Nielsen:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the
campaign provisions ofthe Political Reform Act (the.Act,').l
QUESTTONS
Must major donors amend Form 461 if informed that a candidate has kansferred
the contribution to a committee for a different office and attributed the transfer to that
contributor?2
CONCLUSIONS
Major donors need not amend Form 46I inthe circumstances you describe.
FACTS
A client of your firm, Edvoice, is currently making contributions to controlled
committees of incumbent state legislators. You report the name of the committee and the
office being sought on the clients' next major donor reports. After their Form 461,s are
filed, some of these contributors have received notices that legislators have decided to
Re
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl0l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of tle California Code of Regulations.
. i "9* 
request initially asked about the advice to contributors to pre-34 committees. That
question has been withdrawn in light of recent Commission action regarding regulation 1 853 1.6 and
emergency regulation I 853 1.61.
B-l


File No. A-03-255
Page No. 2
seek a different office and have attributed the clients' contributions to different campaign
committees. You ask whether the major donor is required to amend his or her report.
ANALYSIS
Your question asks whether major donors must amend Form 461 when the donor
is notified that a candidate to whom the donor has made a contribution has subsequently
transferred that contribution to a new committee belonging to the same candidate for a
different office. So long as the initial Form 461 is u"*uti when filed, we find no such
requirement in the Act that the major donor amend his or her form in the circumstances
you describe. So long as the contributor maintains accurate records and does not exceed
contribution limits, the major donor's obligations are satisfied. (Reg. 1g536;r"Ua. 1g1.;
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.3
Sincerely,
Luisa
General
By:
Scott
Enclosure
CSTjg

Counsel, Legal Division

tre recently adopted emergency regulation 1853i.61, gor"-i"t "ortiiutiom to pre-proposition 34
committees.
